title: I Am Allowed to Forgive Myself
subtitle: An affirmation for releasing the ongoing sentence for past choices
excerpt: A short affirmation for self-forgiveness: the permission to stop carrying a past action as an ongoing verdict on who you are. For the pattern of holding your own behaviour to a permanent account.

practice.whenToUse: Use when a past choice or action is still running as a background accusation, particularly when it is no longer connected to any current behaviour that needs changing.

content.text: A short affirmation for ending the ongoing sentence. Self-criticism after a mistake serves a function: it registers that something went wrong. When it continues long after the registering is done, it shifts from information to punishment. This affirmation is for the point at which the case can close.
Say it three times, slowly, hand on your chest:
I am allowed to forgive myself. The case is closed. I continue.

Three sentences. The first is permission: forgiveness is available to you, not just to others. The second is a formal closing: the account is settled. The third is the action that follows: not absolution, not forgetting, but continuing.
